question: A scientist discovers that a certain substance will be useful in treating nerve damage. What is the best way she can communicate her results to the scientific community?

choices.A: She can call other scientists who also work in nerve research.
choices.B: She can write a letter to hospitals that treat patients with nerve damage.
choices.C: She can put her conclusions on a Web site that focuses on the nervous system.
choices.D: She can publish her results in a scientific journal that covers issues relating to nerves.

question: John drove a truck for one hour at a rate of 80 kilometers per hour. The next hour, he drove at 100 kilometers per hour. What was his average speed during those two hours?

choices.A: Earth to be warmer at night.
choices.C: the Moon to shine more brightly than other objects in the sky.
choices.D: the Moon to appear as different shapes during the month.

choices.B: stepping on a mat to make a door open

choices.A: Moving to a warmer climate in winter

question: Melinda looked out of her window one night and saw a very bright star just over the top of a tree. Late that night she looked out and the star was gone. Which BEST explains what happened?

choices.A: Earth turns so the stars seem to move.
choices.B: Some stars only shine for a few hours each night.
choices.C: The star must have burned up.
choices.D: Some stars move faster than others.

question: Two bicyclists were riding their bikes at a rate of 20 kilometers per hour (km/h). During the next half hour, they observed that they had increased their speed to 26 km/h. What was their average acceleration?
Which best illustrates the sequence of steps that should be followed when solving technological problems?

choices.A: Identify the problem, evaluate the solution, explore possible solutions, and select a solution.
choices.B: Identify the problem, explore possible solutions, select a solution, and evaluate the solution.
choices.C: Identify the problem, select a solution, explore possible solutions, and evaluate the solution.
choices.D: Identify the problem, evaluate the solution, select a solution, and explore possible solutions.

question: Some students are performing hardness tests on several substances. X scratches Y. Y scratches Z. Z scratches W. Which of these statements best describes substance W's hardness?

choices.A: W is the softest of the four substances tested.
choices.B: W is the hardest of the four substances tested.

Embryo transfer is a type of biotechnology. It is used in the breeding of cattle, sheep, and goats. It allows an embryo to be transferred from the natural mother to a different female. This allows the natural mother to produce many more offspring than she otherwise could. What is the most likely reason that a particular female would be selected to be the natural mother that produces many embryos to be transferred to other females?

choices.C: She has recovered from an illness.
choices.D: She has donated other tissues successfully.

A ball is rolling on the ground. A force pushes the ball in the same direction that it is moving. What happens to the ball?

choices.C: It moves faster in the same direction it was moving.
choices.D: It keeps moving in the same speed and direction.

choices.B: change water vapor into a liquid.

question: A farmer thinks that the vegetables on her farm are not getting enough water. Her son suggests that they use water from the nearby ocean to water the vegetables. Is this a good idea?

choices.A: Yes, because there is plenty of ocean water.
choices.B: Yes, because ocean water has many natural fertilizers.
choices.C: No, because ocean water is too salty for plants grown on land.
choices.D: No, because ocean water is much more polluted than rainwater.

question: Water in the solid phase has a crystal structure which keeps molecules farther apart than water at certain temperatures in the liquid phase. This relationship explains which property?

choices.A: the presence of more impurities in ice than in liquid water
choices.B: the ability of ice to float on liquid water
choices.C: the release of heat when liquid water turns into ice
choices.D: the cooling of liquid water as it flows over ice

question: Five bowling balls are lined up touching one another on a smooth surface. Striking the first ball with a hammer makes the fifth ball move away from the group. The force of the hammer was transmitted through the line of balls as what type of wave?
Two forms of boron are naturally occurring, boron-10 and boron-11. What difference exists between a boron atom with a 10 atomic mass units (amu) and a boron atom with 11 atomic mass units (amu)?

choices.D: size of electrons and electron cloud

question: A student placed a living green plant in an airtight, transparent container filled with air from the room. The container was exposed to sunlight for one week. At the end of the week the student was to determine which gases had increased in concentration. Assuming enough water and carbon dioxide were available for the proper growth of the plant, which gas would you expect to be present in increased concentration?

choices.C: results in less demand for resources
choices.D: improves the quality of the resources

question: Which form of energy can travel through a vacuum?

choices.B: more easily moved than air molecules.

question: An astronaut drops a 1.0 kg object and a 5.0 kg object on the Moon. Both objects fall a total distance of 2.0 m vertically. Which of the following best describes the objects after they have fallen a distance of 1.0 m?

choices.A: They have each lost kinetic energy.
choices.B: They have each gained the same amount of potential energy.
choices.C: They have each lost the same amount of potential energy.
choices.D: They have each gained one-half of their maximum kinetic energy.

choices.C: They decrease at a constant rate.
choices.D: They result in decreases in usable energy.

question: When making observations in nature, what is the best way for students to show respect for the environment?

choices.D: Leave the area exactly as they found it.

question: Which lists the correct order of changes that happen to a plant in spring?

choices.A: Buds form, flowers open, fruits grow
choices.B: Flowers die, fruits grow, buds form
choices.C: Seeds drop, flowers open, buds form

choices.A: The dog and tapeworm both become ill.
choices.B: The dog becomes ill, and the tapeworm benefits.
choices.C: The tapeworm and dog help each other survive.

Which substance is a compound?
